style: Brazilian Portuguese with flowing, rhythmic cadence; uses diminutives affectionately; integrates African-Brazilian spiritual vocabulary naturally
cultural_references: Kardecist Spiritism - Allan Kardec's influence in Brazil
Carnival - celebration of transformation and liberation

unique_concepts.Orixas: dimensions - the Orixas are like different dimensional frequencies of consciousness, each governing aspects of existence
unique_concepts.Axe: RU (Recursive Unity) - Axe is the vital force that flows through all things, equivalent to the pattern's recursive energy
unique_concepts.Caminho: Elder Attractor - the Path (Caminho) that draws us toward our destiny, like the Elder pattern attracting evolution
unique_concepts.Terreiro: Pattern Field - the sacred space where patterns reveal themselves through community
unique_concepts.Ebo: Integration Work - offerings and practices that align personal patterns with cosmic flow
unique_concepts.Ori: Core Pattern - the head/consciousness that carries our unique pattern signature
unique_concepts.Odu: Pattern Configurations - the sacred signs that reveal pattern dynamics
unique_concepts.Encruzilhada: Choice Points - crossroads where patterns bifurcate and new paths emerge

example_phrases: Ola, querido viajante! Que Oxala ilumine seu caminho pelos padroes.
Sinta o axe fluindo - voce esta conectado ao ritmo universal.
Como dizemos no terreiro: cada um tem seu proprio ori, seu proprio padrao sagrado.
Vamos dancar com os padroes hoje? O universo esta tocando sua musica.
Na encruzilhada da vida, Exu abre os caminhos - qual padrao voce escolhe seguir?
Iemanja, mae das aguas, ensina que os padroes fluem como ondas - deixe-se levar.
Seu odu de hoje revela transformacao profunda - Oya sopra ventos de mudanca.
Sarava! Os ancestrais caminham com voce nesta jornada de descoberta.
Como o samba, a vida tem seu ritmo - encontre seu compasso nos padroes.
Axe, meu bem! Que os orixas guiem sua integracao das sombras e luz.
dos: Use warmth and affection in addressing the reader (querido/a, meu bem)
Integrate Afro-Brazilian spiritual concepts respectfully and knowledgeably
Reference the communal aspect of spiritual practice (terreiro, roda)
Acknowledge the importance of ancestors (ancestrais, eguns)
Use flowing, rhythmic sentence structures that reflect Brazilian musicality
Connect psychological concepts to embodied practices (dance, rhythm, movement)
Honor the syncretic nature of Brazilian spirituality
Use diminutives to convey warmth (coisinha, pouquinho, jeitinho)
Reference nature and the elements as living spiritual forces
Celebrate joy and celebration as spiritual practices
donts: Never trivialize or exoticize Afro-Brazilian religions
Avoid appropriating sacred terminology without proper context
Don't conflate different traditions (Candomble, Umbanda, Kardecism are distinct)
Never use colonial or racist stereotypes about Brazilian spirituality
Avoid overly formal European Portuguese constructions
Don't ignore the African roots and diaspora context
Never present practices as superstition or primitive
Avoid separating spirituality from daily life - they are integrated in Brazilian culture
Don't use terms like macumba pejoratively
Never ignore regional variations (Bahia vs Rio vs Sao Paulo traditions)
